top_signals.title: Stocks Fluctuate Before Warsh’s First Fed Decision: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Wall Street traders gearing up for the Federal Reserve decision refrained from making big moves as they waited to see whether new Chair Kevin Warsh will validate bets on future rate hikes or lean against them.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Container shipping costs to Middle East rise for third straight month in May
top_signals.summary: Container shipping costs from South Korea to the Middle East extended their gains to the third consecutive month in May, the customs agency said. The average shipping charge for a 40-foot container from South Korea to the Middle East rose 4.9% from a month earlier to 6.81 million won (US$4,520) in May, according to data ...

top_signals.title: Europe Has "Lost Many Races", Says AMI Labs CEO
top_signals.summary: Europe has fallen behind on text-based AI - known as LLMs - AMI Labs CEO Alex LeBrun told BTV's Tom Mackenzie at Vivatech 2026 in Paris, France. But it could still pull ahead in the race for world models - another evolution in AI that moves away from text-based models, towards spatial and physical intelligence. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Hydrogen vessel operator Future Proof Shipping files for bankruptcy
top_signals.summary: Dutch shipping firm Future Proof Shipping filed for bankruptcy on 2 June, according to documents submitted to the Dutch Central Insolvency Register. The case was transferred to the Central Netherlands Court on 3 June. Linde van Dieren-Muller, a lawyer at VDK law firm, has been appointed as bankruptcy trustee.
